channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Ruling may further delay count of Puerto Rico's still-uncertified votes in Nov. 5 elections
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — The U.S. territory of Puerto Rico has yet to produce a certified vote tally for a single district almost a month after holding historic general elections.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US tells Ukraine to 'look hard' at lowering draft age in Russia war
item.description: The United States is urging Ukraine to “look hard” at removing policy mandates that limit the nation to drafting soldiers aged 25 and up, a senior administration official told The Hill on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: 5 key takeaways from Israel-Lebanon ceasefire brokered by US
item.description: President Biden’s announcement Tuesday of a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon marked a significant foreign policy achievement weeks before he leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Democrat calls for sanctions over violent clashes in Pakistan
item.description: Rep. Ro Khanna (D-Calif.) denounced Pakistan’s chief of army staff Wednesday for allegedly killing citizens and confiscating death records from medics in an effort to subdue growing protests in the nation’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump taps Keith Kellogg as special envoy to Ukraine and Russia
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced his appointment of Keith Kellogg, a retired lieutenant general who served as chief of staff on Trump’s National Security Council during his first term, as special envoy to Ukraine and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: NATO chief: Ukraine not in strong position to negotiate with Putin
item.description: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said on Wednesday that Ukraine is not in a strong enough position to begin negotiating an end to the war with Russia and said any deal struck must “prevent the Russians from getting what they want.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: White House pressing Ukraine to draft 18-year-olds so it has enough troops to battle Russia
item.description: President Joe Biden’s administration is urging Ukraine to quickly increase the size of its military by drafting more troops and revamping its mobilization laws to allow for the conscription of those as young as 18.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US issues new sanctions on Maduro allies in Venezuela over fraudulent elections
item.description: The Biden administration is sanctioning nearly two dozen Venezuelan security and Cabinet officials aligned with President Nicolás Maduro, building on a series of sanctions it has imposed along with other countries following July elections the U.S. deems illegitimate.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: 2,000-year-old fig unearthed in Ireland marks ‘oldest example of an exotic fruit’ discovered in the area
item.description: During excavations in Ireland, an ancient fig dating back 2,000 years was unearthed. The finding of the fig marks a fist-of-its kind discovery for Ireland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire takes effect with region on edge
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah took effect early Wednesday morning, after the Israeli and Lebanese governments approved a U.S. proposal to halt the fighting in a truce that President Biden called a permanent cessation of hostilities. Ahead of the ceasefire deadline, 4 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: China releases 3 Americans in prisoner swap
item.description: Mark Swidan, Kai Li and John Leung were freed after years of talks between U.S. and Chinese officials.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Concerns over housing and immigration make for a volatile campaign in Ireland's election
item.description: DUBLIN (AP) — From his poster, there’s not much to distinguish Gerry Hutch from the slew of other independent candidates hoping discontented voters will propel them into Ireland’s parliament in an election on Friday. “We need change, and I’m your man,” it says.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire quiets one front but Gaza sees no end to war
item.description: For many across the Middle East, the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire came as a relief: the first major sign of progress in the region since war began more than a year ago.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: China releases 3 'wrongfully detained' Americans, White House says
item.description: China is releasing three Americans who were "wrongfully detained," a National Security Council spokesperson says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biggest November snowstorm in half century hits Seoul and grounds flights
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The biggest November snowstorm to hit South Korea’s capital in more than a half century blanketed the capital on Wednesday, grounding hundreds of flights, disrupting commuter traffic and leaving at least two dead.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Philippine police file criminal complaints against Sara Duterte and her security aides
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine police officials on Wednesday filed criminal complaints against Vice President Sara Duterte and her security staff for allegedly assaulting authorities and disobeying orders in a recent altercation in Congress.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about the ceasefire deal between Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah
item.description: A ceasefire deal that went into effect on Wednesday could end more than a year of cross-border fighting between Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah militant group, raising hopes and renewing difficult questions in a region gripped by conflict. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: ADAM GELLER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: An Australia police officer who shocked a 95-year-old woman with a Taser is guilty of manslaughter
item.description: A police officer who shocked a 95-year-old nursing home resident with a Taser was found guilty of manslaughter in an Australian court.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: G7 ministers throw support behind Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire but make no mention of ICC warrant
item.description: FIUGGI, Italy (AP) — Foreign ministers from leading industrialized countries threw their strong support Tuesday behind an immediate ceasefire between Israel and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah but sidestepped the question of whether to enforce an international arrest warrant for Israel’s leade
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philippine investigators summon VP Duterte over her public threats against President Marcos
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine authorities handed a subpoena to Vice President Sara Duterte’s office Tuesday, inviting her to answer investigators' questions after she publicly threatened to have the president, his wife and the House of Representatives speaker assassinated if she were killed
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden team briefed Trump transition on Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire
item.description: President-elect Trump's transition team has received briefings from the chief architect of the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire deal under President Biden, a senior administration official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Israel, Lebanon's Hezbollah agree to a ceasefire to end nearly 14 months of fighting
item.description: Israel approved a United States-brokered ceasefire agreement with Lebanon's Hezbollah on Tuesday, setting the stage for an end to nearly 14 months of fighting linked to the ongoing war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New subway system in Greece puts archaeological finds discovered during construction on display for passengers
item.description: A new subway system is on its way to being showcased to passengers in Thessaloniki, Greece. The project unveiled thousands of ancient finds through excavations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bangladeshi court denies bail to Hindu leader who led rallies for minority protection
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — A prominent Bangladeshi Hindu leader who has been leading rallies demanding security for Hindus in the overwhelmingly Muslim nation was ordered detained on charges of sedition Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel launches new airstrikes on Lebanon as leaders draw closer to a ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel's military launched airstrikes across Lebanon on Monday, unleashing explosions throughout the country and killing at least 31 while Israeli leaders appeared to be closing in on a negotiated ceasefire with the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAM METZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Amelia Earhart’s plane photo not of the plane, company says
item.description: “After 11 months the waiting has finally ended and unfortunately our target was not Amelia's Electra 10E," the exploration company said.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Israel 'moving forward' on possible Hezbollah cease-fire, official says
item.description: The Israeli Security Cabinet will convene Tuesday to discuss a possible cease-fire agreement with Hezbollah terrorists in Lebanon, Ambassador Danny Danon said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Drones detected near 3 US bases in England last week: Air Force
item.description: Between Wednesday and Friday, multiple unidentified drones were detected near RAF Lakenheath, RAF Mildenhall and RAF Feltwell.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Shootout near Israeli Embassy in Jordan leaves 3 police officers injured, gunman dead
item.description: Police in Jordan shot and killed a gunman who wounded three police officers near the Israeli Embassy on Sunday morning, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rabbi feared kidnapped, killed by terrorists after going missing, prompting investigation
item.description: Rabbi Zvi Kogan, a Chabad emissary, is feared dead after "terrorist involvement" was found related to his disappearance Thursday. Israeli and Emirati officials are investigating.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
